title: leea asiatica
intro: Leea asiatica, also known as Leea rubra or Leea indica, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Leea asiatica include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders. Herbal preparations of Leea asiatica include tea, tincture, and infusion, which can be used to support immune function and overall well-being.
